reviews.review_description: This is a passive flat screen view of a walking tour with weird graphics under where you stand. Very underwhelming. I've been to Florence and Venice and this detracts from the experience entirely.

developer_response.body:
Thank you for your feedback.

This app is not meant to be a guided tour or a game — it’s a calm VR walk through real places, without narration or tasks. The visuals capture the authentic lighting of a Florentine sunset, and the motion is intentionally slow to reflect the pace of a real evening stroll.

If you were looking for something more action-driven, this might feel different. But many users value exactly this sense of slowness — being present, hearing the city, seeing the architecture, without pressure or distraction.

We hope other visitors will discover the quiet depth this experience offers.

reviews.review_description:
What a wanderful idea to share this travel experience this way. Immerse into the swinging streets in Florence, feel the vibe of this ancient and vibrant city. To linger on the Piazza della Signoria in front of the Palazzo Vecchio is really cool: admiring the surroundig architecture, prominent edifices, statues, ancient horse-drawn carriages, and seeing all the people, mostly tourists immersed in their own perceptions. 
I have used earbuds for a better immersion.

The cultural richness and modern touch makes Florence to my next italian destination.
